title: Causal hierarchical target stats instead of transductive category frequencies
summary: Your current best code factorizes categories and computes frequency features on train+test together, which is transductive and can make grouped CV look better than deployment.
rationale: Your current best code factorizes categories and computes frequency features on train+test together, which is transductive and can make grouped CV look better than deployment. CatBoost’s ordered-statistics literature exists precisely because naive category statistics create prediction shift/leakage. A safer adjacent-competition pattern is hierarchical shrinkage encoding computed only from past events or OOF folds.
implementation_hint: Drop train+test frequency/code features for Driver/Race/Compound interactions. Build ordered or OOF target stats with Bayesian shrinkage for Driver, Driver-Compound, Race-Compound, and Year-Driver using only earlier races within each fold; back off rare keys to parent levels. Compare grouped CV vs forward-embargo CV to see if small lifts survive.
expected_effect: Usually lowers validation optimism and can improve LB robustness; may also help rare drivers/races where raw factor codes are noisy.
risk: If the leaderboard distribution matches train closely, removing transductive stats can reduce local CV before improving generalization.
